key_insight: The paper proposes a split iterative transient FEM approach for nonlinear eddy-current problems, solving the field and circuit equations separately with Newton-based updates and line search stabilization.
engineering_relevance: This enables more robust time-domain simulation of voltage-driven coil systems with nonlinear magnetic materials, such as laminated iron-core devices under inrush conditions.
method: Implicit Euler time stepping for the transient magnetic vector-potential formulation
Decompose the nonlinear system into a finite element field problem and a circuit equation
Solve each subproblem separately with Newton's method; use line search for the nonlinear field solve
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on a nonlinear benchmark with a laminated iron core in a cylindrical coil, reporting inrush currents, average flux densities, and numerical performance data.
watch_item: Accuracy and convergence depend on the nonlinear solve strategy and benchmark-specific setup, so careful tuning may be needed for different coil-circuit configurations or material laws.
paper_title: An Iterative Method for Transient Finite Element Simulations of Non-Linear Eddy Current Problems
